ogTitle: Musselroe Bay vs Myrtle Bank: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Musselroe Bay and Myrtle Bank.
heading: Musselroe Bay vs Myrtle Bank — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Musselroe Bay, Tasmania and Myrtle Bank, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Musselroe Bay and Myrtle Bank have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Musselroe Bay or Myrtle Bank safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Musselroe Bay has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Myrtle Bank scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Musselroe Bay scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Myrtle Bank scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Musselroe Bay or Myrtle Bank?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Musselroe Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
